title: Tool execution: no tracing spans, no structured logs, no metrics — agent runs are opaque
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Observability / Error Orchestration

**Location:** All execute_* functions in /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/ — bash/mod.rs, file/{read,write,edit,list,notebook}.rs, chainlink.rs, web.rs, worktree.rs, cron.rs, task.rs, ask_user.rs, plan_mode.rs, lsp.rs

**Violation:**
Only MCP (mcp.rs) uses tracing::{debug, info, warn}. Every other tool path is silent:
1. No tracing::info_span!("bash", command = %cmd) — impossible to correlate a tool call with downstream provider requests or database queries.
2. No metrics (prometheus, opentelemetry). Cannot measure p99 latency of read_file, bash timeouts, cron execution frequency, or denial rate from the permission manager.
3. No structured error logging. All errors bubble as (String, true); no 'tool_failure' counter exists to alert on.
4. tool_intercept.rs line 607 prints '\n\x1b[36m⚡ Running {} locally...\x1b[0m' with println! — directly to stdout with ANSI escapes. This is a debug aid that shouldn't be in production paths; it pollutes piped output and doesn't respect RUST_LOG.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Wrap every execute_* in #[tracing::instrument(skip(args), fields(tool = %NAME))] — automatic span per call.
2. Emit a structured tracing::info event at span close with {duration_ms, is_error, output_size}.
3. Replace println!-with-escape at tool_intercept.rs:607 with tracing::info!. Output colouring belongs in the TUI layer, not the tool layer.
4. Expose counters via metrics crate: openclaudia.tool.calls_total {tool, outcome}; openclaudia.tool.duration_seconds {tool}.
5. Document minimum RUST_LOG=openclaudia=info to observe tool activity.
